text: Urban traffic congestion is a ubiquitous problem worldwide, leading to waste of oil, loss of working efficiency, and increased likelihood of accidents caused by delay in emergency service. Generally, existing urban traffic management systems are based on Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) with fixed static round-robin scheduling algorithms, which are unable to meet the varying demand of traffic in real time. At present, some intelligent traffic management systems based on IoT have implemented control based on the traffic density in the current section of the road, but the quality of the datasets on emergency vehicles used for training the deep learning model is scarce. In this paper, a new distributed architecture for IoT in intelligent traffic management is proposed based on two aspects. On the one hand, taking the relatively stable condition of the urban roads into account, the vehicle detection algorithm is designed into two stages that first utilize the background subtraction technology, and then the detected objects are further identified using deep learning based on the UA-DETRAC dataset, and the accuracy of the detection result can reach 77\%. On the other hand, according to the characteristics of emergency vehicles that can be identified by the different acoustic signals they produce, an siren detection algorithm is designed for the edge devices, and finally achieves a detection accuracy of 99.4\%. Ultimately, the two stages achieve the goal of efficient intelligent traffic management and optimal emergency route planning.
